mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces corporate borrowing costs and compresses equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate above 4.5 percent signals labor market deterioration and impending recession, causing institutional investors to sell equities and producing negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in more than 55 percent of instances when UNRATE crosses the 4.5 percent threshold over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, driving corporate earnings growth and generating positive equity returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns with a statistically significant coefficient (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in industrial production signals supply-side bottlenecks and reduced economic activity, triggering short-term risk-off positioning and negative equity returns within one week.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines are followed by positive 5-day returns in more than 60 percent of cases during monetary tightening cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation combined with a rising federal funds rate indicates a hawkish central bank response that increases discount rates and compresses equity multiples, leading to negative forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising CPI and rising rates yields a positive 20-day return in more than 50 percent of triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic capacity and wage growth, supporting corporate revenue and generating positive equity returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no statistically significant correlation with 60-day forward returns (Rank IC < 0.01) across rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly decline in real GDP signals economic contraction, reducing corporate profit expectations and triggering negative equity returns over the following month.
failure_condition: GDP contractions are followed by positive 20-day returns in more than 65 percent of instances over a 15-year historical sample.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between falling industrial production and rising unemployment confirms a broad economic slowdown, prompting rapid portfolio de-risking and negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: The divergence signal fails to predict negative 5-day returns with a win rate below 40 percent over a 10-year sample.
